payload.level:
The ideal practice to flow into the rest of the day, feeling refreshed and energized. This morning vinyasa flow gently awakens the body. 

During a vinyasa practice we synchronize movement and breath in order to 'flow' with ease, flowing fluidly from one pose to another. This harmonizes body and mind, while at the same time building strength and flexibility. A lovely start of the day.

This vinyasa flow is a nice way for beginners to get a taste of vinyasa flow yoga. 

Are you pregnant or do you have a specific injury? Let the teacher know in advance so they can guide you.

Level of this class: basic - all level

Heads up! Paying online? Choose iDEAL to avoid extra fees. Other payment options may charge higher transaction fees.

Tai Chi is a gentle way to move your body, calm your mind, and feel more balanced. It’s a moving meditation. Slow, flowing movements help you find focus, strength, and peace from within.

Rooted in ancient Taoist philosophy, Taijiquan or Tai Chi is originally an internal martial arts form, all about the balance between Yin and Yang. Today, many people practice it for its healing and calming effects.

Tai Chi helps your life energy — also called Qi — flow more freely through your body. This creates a tangible connection between mind, body and spirit that brings clarity and improves focus. It can naturally ease tension, pain, or stress, and help you feel more grounded and alive.

Tai Chi is part of the larger Qigong tradition. There are several styles, and we practice the Yang style — the most popular and accessible form, perfect for beginners and advanced students alike.

Come as you are. No experience needed — just an open mind and a willingness to move.
Level of this class: All levels

Heads up! Paying online? Choose iDEAL to avoid extra fees. Other payment options may charge higher transaction fees.

A complete yoga practice that combines dynamic and static asanas,
infused with breathwork to calm the mind, boost energy and vitality,
and tap into the inner power that exists in all of us. 
The class concludes with a brief meditation that balances the mind and guides you toward a higher state of awareness.

A complete yoga practice that combines dynamic and static asanas,
infused with breathwork to calm the mind, boost energy and vitality,
and tap into the inner power that exists in all of us. 
The class concludes with a brief meditation that balances the mind and guides you toward a higher state of awareness.

Yin Yoga

In contrast to more dynamic forms of yoga, Yin yoga doesn't aim to strengthen the muscles, but to relax them. By relaxing our muscles, we target our connective tissues in the deeper layers of the body. It takes longer to stretch and strengthen connective tissue than muscle tissue. For that reason, all yin yoga poses are held for up to 5 minutes. Yin yoga relaxes (even though it might also challenge you in a good way), stimulates the flow of energy through our meridians (energy lines) and organs and creates space in body and mind.

Please always inform the teacher in case you're pregnant. If you've just given birth, we advise not to practice yin for at least 3 months to give the body time to strengthen again.

Level of this class: basic - all level

Studio or online: follow your class in the studio or online, wherever you are.

Yoga mats, meditation cushions, belts, blankets and blocks are all provided for you in the studio. You’ll also find dressing rooms, lockers and showers in the school. Before or after your class you’re very welcome in the living room or courtyard garden of DNYS café for a tea, coffee, homemade soup or (vegan) snack. Thank you for arriving at least 10 minutes before your studio class starts.

Are you booking an online class? Then you can attend the class ‘live’ in the livestream or access the replay up to 48 hours after the class. You’ll find the online classes that you booked in your account.

Heads up! Paying online? Choose iDEAL to avoid extra fees. Other payment options may charge higher transaction fees.

Healing Yoga is a combination of different practices (like Yoga Therapy, Yin, Restorative Yoga, Breathwork, Self-Inquiry, Yoga Nidra, Somatics) that relax, restore and strengthen the whole system.

Healing yoga is a gentle form of (trauma) informed yoga. In a safe setting, you experience the space to feel through any emotions and wounds that may live in you and to connect to who you truly are. Using different practical tools, we go on an inner journey to embrace what is. The light and the dark. When you experience stress in the body, simple yoga-, somatic-, and breathwork techniques can bring you back into a state of relaxation and peace. Sometimes, we just need to ‘shake it off’. Sometimes, we’ll use deep slow breathing to effortlessly bring us back into a state of being and feeling. In this way, you can safely feel through any emotions, allow yourself to be vulnerable, bring space to blockages and recognize patterns. When we stop fighting and start to feel, our nervous system can restore to a state of inner balance and that’s where the healing takes place. In essence, ‘healing’ is to experience your wholeness and to return to a state of natural inner harmony.

This class is very suitable for you if you: are pregnant experience burnout symptoms have injuries experience feelings of depression

Level of this class: basic - all level

Heads up! Paying online? Choose iDEAL to avoid extra fees. Other payment options may charge higher transaction fees.

This class provides a safe, supportive space to release tension and connect with yourself. A Vinyasa Flow for all, that connects breath and movement, creating a fluid practice where each pose transitions seamlessly into the next. This dynamic flow becomes a moving meditation, helping you feel more grounded and connected while building strength, flexibility, and overall vitality.

Accessible for all levels, this class is a great way to improve both body and mind. 

If you’re pregnant or have an injury, please inform the teacher before class for personalized guidance.

Level of this class: Intermediate - Advanced

Heads up! Paying online? Choose iDEAL to avoid extra fees. Other payment options may charge higher transaction fees.
